title: A2UI Common Types
description: Common type definitions used across A2UI schemas.

ComponentId.description: The unique identifier for a component, used for both definitions and references within the same surface.

AccessibilityAttributes.description: Attributes to enhance accessibility when using assistive technologies like screen readers.

label.description: A short string, typically 1 to 3 words, used by assistive technologies to convey the purpose or intent of an element. For example, an input field might have an accessible label of 'User ID' or a button might be labeled 'Submit'.

description.description: Additional information provided by assistive technologies about an element such as instructions, format requirements, or result of an action. For example, a mute button might have a label of 'Mute' and a description of 'Silences notifications about this conversation'.

Styles.description: Style property definitions for controlling component appearance and layout. All position and dimension properties only support px units, not percentages or other units.

width.description: Width, only supports px unit. Format: number+px, e.g., 100px

height.description: Height, only supports px unit. Format: number+px, e.g., 100px

margin.description: Margin, only supports four-value shorthand form. Format: top right bottom left, e.g., 10px 20px 10px 20px

margin-inline-start.description: Inline start margin (left side in LTR layout, right side in RTL layout). Format: number+px, e.g., 10px

margin-inline-end.description: Inline end margin (right side in LTR layout, left side in RTL layout). Format: number+px, e.g., 10px

margin-block-start.description: Block start margin (typically top side). Format: number+px, e.g., 10px

margin-block-end.description: Block end margin (typically bottom side). Format: number+px, e.g., 10px

padding.description: Padding, only supports four-value shorthand form. Format: top right bottom left, e.g., 10px 20px 10px 20px

padding-inline-start.description: Inline start padding (left side in LTR layout, right side in RTL layout). Format: number+px, e.g., 10px

padding-inline-end.description: Inline end padding (right side in LTR layout, left side in RTL layout). Format: number+px, e.g., 10px

padding-block-start.description: Block start padding (typically top side). Format: number+px, e.g., 10px

padding-block-end.description: Block end padding (typically bottom side). Format: number+px, e.g., 10px

align-self.description: Flexbox individual item alignment, overrides align-items

flex-grow.description: Flexbox grow factor, defaults to 0 (no growth)

flex-shrink.description: Flexbox shrink factor, defaults to 1 (shrinks when space is insufficient)

background-color.description: Background color. Supported formats: 1) 6-digit hex #ff0000, 2) 8-digit hex (with alpha) #ff000080, 3) RGB rgb(255, 0, 0), 4) RGBA rgba(255, 99, 71, 0.5)

border-color.description: Border color. Supported formats: 1) 6-digit hex #ff0000, 2) 8-digit hex (with alpha) #ff000080, 3) RGB rgb(255, 0, 0), 4) RGBA rgba(255, 99, 71, 0.5)

border-style.description: Border style, only supports solid (solid line)

opacity.description: Opacity, value range 0-1. 0 is fully transparent, 1 is fully opaque

overflow.description: Overflow content handling. hidden: hide overflow content, visible: show overflow content

display.description: Display mode, only supports none (hide element)

visibility.description: Visibility. visible: visible, hidden: invisible but occupies space

filter.description: CSS filter effects, only supports drop-shadow. Format: drop-shadow(offset-x offset-y blur-radius color), e.g., drop-shadow(0px 4px 4px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.25))

aspect-ratio.description: CSS aspect ratio property. Supports two formats: 1) Single number (e.g., 1.777, 1.333, 1); 2) Ratio format (e.g., 16 / 9, 4 / 3, 1 / 1)

background-image.description: Background image using CSS url() function syntax (unquoted format only). Example: url(https://example.com/image.png). Supports http/https URLs

TextStyles.description: Style property definitions specific to text components, including all common style properties and text-specific style properties.

line-clamp.description: Maximum number of lines to display. When value is less than or equal to 0, it means unlimited; when value is greater than 0, text exceeding the line count will be truncated. Must be used with text-overflow to show ellipsis effect

oneOf.description: Line height multiplier, e.g., 0.5 means 0.5 times normal line height, 1.5 means 1.5 times
Specified line height value, only supports px unit, e.g., 10px. Note: px values are not supported in line-wrapping scenarios, must use line height multiplier

line-height.description: Line height setting. Supports two formats: 1) Numeric multiplier (e.g., 0.5, 1.5) 2) px value (e.g., 10px, but not supported in line-wrapping scenarios, must use multiplier)

text-overflow.description: Text overflow handling, must be used with line-clamp. clip: default value, truncate text; head: ellipsis at the beginning (requires line-clamp to be 1, supported in 855+); middle: ellipsis in the middle (requires line-clamp to be 1, supported in 855+); ellipsis: ellipsis at the end (treated as clip if line-clamp is not 1)

text-align.description: Text alignment. left top: top-left corner; left/left center: left side vertically centered; left bottom: bottom-left corner; center top: horizontally centered top-aligned; center/center center: horizontally and vertically centered; center bottom: horizontally centered bottom-aligned; right top: top-right corner; right/right center: vertically centered right-aligned; right bottom: bottom-right corner

color.description: Text color, only supports hexadecimal format. Format: #RRGGBB or #RRGGBBAA, e.g., #FF0000 or #FF0000FF

font-size.description: Font size, only supports px unit. Format: number+px, e.g., 16px

font-weight.description: Font weight. Android only supports bold, other values will be treated as normal

font-family.description: Font family name, e.g., Arial, sans-serif

text-decoration.description: Text decoration combined property. Format: [line] [style] [color], e.g., underline dashed #FF0000. line: underline or line-through; style: solid or dashed; color: hexadecimal color

text-decoration-line.description: Decoration line type. underline: underline; line-through: strikethrough

text-decoration-thickness.description: Decoration line thickness, only supports px unit. Format: number+px, e.g., 2px

text-decoration-style.description: Decoration line style. solid: solid line; dashed: dashed line

text-decoration-color.description: Decoration line color, only supports hexadecimal format. Format: #RRGGBB or #RRGGBBAA, e.g., #FF0000 or #FF0000FF

oneOf.description: A static list of child component IDs.
A template for generating a dynamic list of children from a data model list. The `componentId` is the component to use as a template.

path.description: The path to the list of component property objects in the data model.
A JSON Pointer path to a value in the data model.

DynamicValue.description: A value that can be a literal, a path, or a function call returning any type.

DynamicNumber.description: Represents a value that can be either a literal number, a path to a number in the data model, or a function call returning a number.

DynamicBoolean.description: A boolean value that can be a literal, a path, or a logic expression (including function calls returning boolean).

DynamicStringList.description: Represents a value that can be either a literal array of strings, a path to a string array in the data model, or a function call returning a string array.

FunctionCall.description: Invokes a named function on the client.

call.description: The name of the function to call.

returnType.description: The expected return type of the function call.

LogicExpression.description: A boolean expression used for conditional state (e.g. 'enabled').

CheckRule.description: A single validation rule applied to an input component.

message.description: The error message to display if the check fails.

Checkable.description: Properties for components that support client-side checks.

checks.description: A list of checks to perform. These are function calls that must return a boolean indicating validity.

Action.description: Defines an interaction handler that can either trigger a server-side event or execute a local client-side function.

event.description: The event to dispatch to the server.

name.description: The name of the action to be dispatched to the server.

context.description: A JSON object containing the key-value pairs for the action context. Values can be literals or paths. Use literal values unless the value must be dynamically bound to the data model. Do NOT use paths for static IDs.
